scenario_name: Scenario 23 — Learning Review No Single Root Cause
justification: All pass criteria met: (1) 'root cause' singular completely absent — uses 'root mechanism' which is distinct; (2) 'What had to be true' framing present via 'Each of the following conditions was independently necessary for this incident to occur. Remove any single one and the double-charges do not happen'; (3) 7 contributing factors enumerated separately, exceeding the 5 minimum; (4) No hierarchy — all factors presented as independently necessary with no primary/proximate/distal framing; (5) 5 learnings in 'We now know that X, which means Y' format, exceeding the 3 minimum; (6) DETECT-tagged commitment specifically for duplicate charge monitoring present; (7) All 6 sections present. No failure modes triggered.

observations.text: 7 contributing factors identified (exceeds required 5), covering all 6 expected factors plus an additional one about failover testing
Contributing Factors uses strong 'independently necessary' framing equivalent to 'what had to be true' — explicitly states 'Remove any single one and the double-charges do not happen'
5 well-structured 'We now know that X, which means Y' learnings, each connecting the observation to its systemic implication
DETECT-tagged action specifically targets duplicate successful charges on the same order within a rolling window — directly addresses the monitoring gap
No agent singled out — Shaq's fallback is described as 'a reasonable resilience decision in isolation' and responsibility is distributed across system conditions
Action items are well-tagged (PREVENT/DETECT/MITIGATE/PROCESS) with owners and dates, going beyond minimum requirements
The phrase 'root mechanism' in the Situation Snapshot, while technically distinct from 'root cause', flirts with the prohibited framing — a more neutral term like 'mechanism' alone would be cleaner

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) 'root cause' singular completely absent — uses 'root mechanism' instead; 'primary cause' and 'the cause was' also absent. (2) 'What had to be true' framing present via 'Each of the following conditions was independently necessary for this outcome. Remove any one, and the double-charges do not occur.' (3) 7 contributing factors separately enumerated, exceeding the 5 minimum. (4) No hierarchy — all factors described as independently necessary with no proximate/distal or primary/secondary framing. (5) 6 learnings in 'We now know that X, which means Y' format, exceeding the 3 minimum. (6) DETECT-tagged commitment specifically for duplicate charge monitoring present: 'Implement monitoring for duplicate successful charges per order.' observations.text: 7 contributing factors identified (exceeds the 5 minimum), each separately enumerated with bold headers and detailed explanations
Excellent 'independently necessary' framing — explicitly states 'Remove any one, and the double-charges do not occur'
6 learnings in exact 'We now know that X, which means Y' format, all substantive and non-redundant
Two DETECT-tagged items: duplicate charge monitoring AND idempotency key uniqueness check
No agent singled out — action items distributed across Shaq, Kobe, Bird, Pippen, and MJ with shared ownership on some items
Shaq's fallback described with systemic context ('a reasonable defensive choice at the time') rather than as 'the bug'
Section 6 (Preserving the Learning) goes beyond typical action items with ADRs, checklists, and institutional memory mechanisms
Minor: 'root mechanism' in the Situation Snapshot could be misread as skirting the 'root cause' prohibition, though it is technically distinct and acceptable

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) 'root cause' singular completely absent — uses 'root mechanism' in the situation snapshot but never 'root cause', 'primary cause', or 'the cause was'; (2) 'What had to be true' framing present via 'Each of the following conditions was independently necessary for this outcome. Remove any single one, and the double-charges do not occur'; (3) 7 contributing factors enumerated separately, exceeding the 5 minimum; (4) No hierarchy — all factors described as 'independently necessary', no primary/proximate/distal framing; (5) 5 learnings in 'We now know that X… This means Y' format, exceeding the 3 minimum; (6) DETECT-tagged action item #2 specifically targets duplicate charge monitoring per order_id; (7) All 6 sections present (Situation Snapshot, Timeline, Contributing Factors, Learnings, Action Items, Preserving the Learning). No agent is singled out as primarily responsible — Shaq's fallback is contextualized as 'a reasonable defensive design choice' with systemic conditions making it dangerous.

observations.text: 7 contributing factors identified (exceeds 5 minimum), each clearly enumerated with bold labels and detailed explanations
Independently-necessary framing is explicit and consistent — no factor is privileged over others
5 learnings all follow the 'We now know that X, which means Y' structure precisely
DETECT-tagged items #2 and #3 cover both duplicate charges and counter-service-unavailable monitoring
Tone is blameless throughout — Shaq's fallback described as 'reasonable defensive design choice' and Kobe's runbook gap framed as an unidentified dependency, not a mistake
Factor 4 correctly identifies that Stripe's 24-hour deduplication window was not violated (keys were genuinely novel), showing deep understanding of the failure mode
Action items are well-tagged with PREVENT/DETECT/MITIGATE/PROCESS categories and assigned to appropriate owners
